commit.message:
fix: Resolve unauthorized token retrieval errors in notification system

ISSUE:
- Token retrieval was failing even when user was logged in
- localStorage parsing was unreliable
- Notifications throwing 'Unauthorized - please log in again' for all roles

ROOT CAUSE:
- getAuthToken() was relying on localStorage parsing which could fail
- useAuthStore state wasn't being checked directly
- No pre-check before attempting API calls

FIXES:
1. Update token retrieval to use useAuthStore.getState() directly
   - Now gets token from active store state first
   - Falls back to localStorage only as last resort
   - Much more reliable across all roles

2. Add early return checks in API methods
   - loadNotifications() checks for token before attempting call
   - loadUnreadCount() checks for token before attempting call
   - Gracefully returns empty/zero without error logs

3. Improve NotificationBell token checking
   - Uses useAuthStore directly instead of localStorage parsing
   - Cleaner and more reliable token detection
   - Properly handles cases when user is not logged in

4. Better error handling
   - Changed console.warn to console.debug for non-critical cases
   - No more error spam when user logs in/out
   - Clear distinction between auth errors and network errors

TESTING:
- Build succeeds without errors
- All roles should now see notifications properly
- No more 'Unauthorized' spam in console

Files modified:
- apps/web/src/store/notifications.js
- apps/web/src/components/NotificationBell.jsx

commit.message:
Fix PE task assignment race condition: workers use leader assignments only

Root cause: Each PE instance has its own EngineeringTaskIssueManager with a
separate in-memory cache. Worker PEs independently called FindNextAssignableTask()
on their stale caches, grabbing the same tasks the leader had already assigned or
was working on.

Fix:
- Worker PEs now check AssignmentQueue (from IssueAssignmentMessage) first
- Workers only work on tasks explicitly assigned by the leader, never independently
- If no message pending, workers refresh from GitHub and look for tasks assigned to
  their DisplayName (recovery path)
- Leader PE refreshes cache from GitHub before picking tasks to see worker claims
- Added claim validation: verify task isn't already in-progress by another PE
- Added FindAssignedTo() to EngineeringTaskIssueManager for worker task discovery

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(training)!: make LR scheduler configurable through Hydra and clean up optimization config (#986) fx

# Summary

The LR scheduler, optimizer, and learning rate were previously scattered
across every training config preset as duplicated inline blocks, with
the scheduler hardcoded to `CosineLRScheduler` in
`configure_optimizers`. This PR consolidates all optimization
configuration into a dedicated `optimization` config group, makes both
the optimizer and scheduler fully configurable via the standard Hydra
`_target_` pattern, and cleans up the code paths that read these
settings.

# Changes

**`training/config/training/optimization/default.yaml`** (new)
- Single source of truth for `lr`, `optimizer`, `lr_scheduler`, and
`pl_lr_scheduler`
- `lr_scheduler` uses `_target_: timm.scheduler.CosineLRScheduler` —
preserves existing default behaviour
- `pl_lr_scheduler` controls Lightning wrapper settings (interval,
monitor, etc.) independently of the scheduler itself; defaults to
`interval: step`

**`training/config/training/optimization/optimizer/`** (new)
- `adamw.yaml`, `ademamix.yaml`, `zero.yaml` — ready-made optimizer
presets to drop in via override


**`training/config/training/{default,autoencoder,ensemble,lam,multi,stretched}.yaml`**
- Adds `- optimization: default` to defaults list
- Removes duplicated inline `optimizer:` and `lr:` blocks from all
preset configs

**`training/config/training/diffusion.yaml`**
- Adds `- optimization: default`; overrides only
`optimization.optimizer` inline (custom `weight_decay`/`eps`); inherits
`lr`, `lr_scheduler`, and `pl_lr_scheduler` from the default

**`training/train/tasks/base.py`**
- `configure_optimizers` now instantiates both optimizer and scheduler
via `hydra.utils.instantiate`; if `lr_scheduler` is absent or null,
returns the optimizer alone (constant LR)
- Removed private `_create_optimizer_from_config` and
`_create_scheduler` methods
- `lr_scheduler_step` generalised to handle both timm-style schedulers
(`step_update`) and standard PyTorch schedulers
- `self.lr` renamed to `self.effective_lr`; reads from
`config.training.optimization.lr`

**`training/train/tasks/ensforecaster.py`**
- Same `self.lr` → `self.effective_lr` / `config.training.lr.rate` →
`config.training.optimization.lr` update

**`training/train/train.py`**
- `config.training.lr.rate` / `config.training.lr.iterations` →
`config.training.optimization.lr` / `config.training.max_steps`

**`training/schemas/training.py`**
- Removes `LR` class; adds `OptimizationSchema` grouping `lr`,
`optimizer`, `lr_scheduler`, `pl_lr_scheduler`;
`BaseTrainingSchema.optimization` replaces the old `lr` + `optimizer`
fields

**`training/tests/unit/train/test_optimizer.py`**
- Tests rewritten against the `configure_optimizers` public API; private
helpers no longer tested directly

**`training/tests/integration/config/testing_modifications.yaml`**
- `training.lr.{warmup,rate}` → `training.optimization.lr`

# Usage

Disable scheduling (constant LR):
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    lr_scheduler: null
```

Switch to a different scheduler:
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    lr_scheduler:
      _target_: torch.optim.lr_scheduler.ReduceLROnPlateau
      mode: min
      factor: 0.5
      patience: 5
    pl_lr_scheduler:
      monitor: val_loss
      interval: epoch
```

Switch to AdEMAMix optimizer:
```yaml
training:
  optimization:
    optimizer:
      _target_: anemoi.training.optimizers.AdEMAMix.AdEMAMix
      betas: [0.9, 0.95, 0.9999]
      alpha: 8.0
```
or use the preset file: `defaults: [optimization/optimizer: ademamix]`

commit.message:
[wasm][coreclr] Implement webcil V1 support in WebcilConverter and readers (#126709)

PR #126388 bumped the shared webcil version from 0 to 1, we need to
update the rest of webcil ecosystem

## Changes

- **Webcil.cs**: Add `TableBase` field to managed `WebcilHeader` struct
(32 bytes for V1)
- **WebcilConverter.cs**: Parameterize version (default V0), write V0
(28-byte) or V1 (32-byte) header based on `webcilVersion` parameter. Add
version validation and WriteStructure bounds checks.
- **WebcilReader.cs**: Read V0 header first (28 bytes), then
conditionally read extra 4-byte TableBase for V1. Version-aware
`SectionDirectoryOffset`.
- **webcil-loader.c** (Mono): Accept both V0 and V1 headers with proper
bounds checking, use correct header size for section offset calculation.

The WebcilConverter defaults to V0, which is what non-R2R builds use.
The R2R/crossgen2 path (WasmObjectWriter + WebcilEncoder) produces V1
independently. Both CoreCLR and Mono decoders now handle V0 and V1.

## Testing

- CoreCLR browser-wasm library tests: 474/476 passed (2 skipped, 0
failed)
- Mono browser-wasm library tests: 469/476 passed (5 pre-existing
DateTime failures, 0 webcil-related)

Supersedes #126698

commit.message:
Fix AdLib capture: note-offs, volume, instrument fingerprinting

Three critical bugs in OPL capture pattern extraction:

1. Missing note-offs — captured note-off events were ignored entirely,
   so notes played forever. Now emits note=127 (XM note-off) when
   key-off is detected in the tick range.

2. Missing volume — captured carrier TL volume was computed but discarded.
   Now packs volume as Cxx (set volume) command in the pattern cell.

3. Instrument fingerprint included volume (TL) bits — every volume change
   created a new duplicate instrument. Now uses instrumentsMatch() that
   compares only KSL (bits 6-7) from 0x40 registers, ignoring TL (0-5).
   Full register data still stored for playback.

Cache version-resilient hash code on MethodTableAuxiliaryData to fix superlinear behavior with polymorphic recursion (#126534)

`GetVersionResilientTypeHashCode` recomputes hash codes recursively
without memoization. With polymorphic recursion, each level doubles the
number of unique generic instantiations, causing exponential blowup —
`n=31` took ~15s before this fix.

On 64-bit release builds, `MethodTableAuxiliaryData` already had 4 bytes
of implicit alignment padding between the union and `m_pLoaderModule`.

feat(frontend): add tabbed UI and Playwright e2e tests (#20)

## Summary

Fixes the blank frontend (Svelte 5 mount API) and refactors the UI with
tabs. Adds Playwright e2e test suite covering all GUI requirements.

## Changes

### Bug fix
- Replace `new App()` (Svelte 4) with `mount()` (Svelte 5) in `main.ts`
— this was causing the blank page

### Frontend refactor
- Single card with **Create / Edit / Delete / Test redirect** tabs —
only active tab is visible
- Improved `style.css`: tab bar, danger-styled Delete button, result-row
layout

### Playwright e2e tests (26 tests, all passing)
| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `navigation.spec.ts` | Tab switching, mutual exclusivity |
| `create-link.spec.ts` | Fields, redirect codes 301/302/307/308,
success + 400/409 errors |
| `edit-link.spec.ts` | Partial update options, token validation,
401/409 errors |
| `delete-link.spec.ts` | Danger styling, success + 401/404 errors |
| `test-redirect.spec.ts` | Active/inactive links, 404 unknown ID |

All tests mock the API via `page.route()` so they run without a live
backend.

## Running tests
```bash
bash run-ui-tests.sh
```
Requires the frontend running at `http://localhost:5173` (`docker
compose up` or `npm run dev`).

docs: demand chain diagnosis, rescue carb inference, and glycogen pool proxy (EXP-1621–1628)

EXP-1621: Demand chain diagnosis — traces ×5.0 time-integration bug
  in supply-demand model. demand/expected ratio = 0.47× (variable).

EXP-1622: Steady-state balance — β-correction fixes fasting R² but
  catastrophically worsens other contexts (meal R² → -16.6).

EXP-1623: Rescue carb inference — median 16g unannounced (n=1,605),
  52% exceed standard 15g dose, 9% meal-sized (>40g). Severe hypos
  get 29% more rescue carbs than mild.

EXP-1624: Per-patient β fails cross-context (confirms EXP-1614).

EXP-1625: Rescue absorption peaks at 10min post-nadir (unannounced),
  20min (announced). 63 mg/dL total effect in first hour.

EXP-1626: Glycogen pool proxy from observable signals. ANOVA F=1207
  (glycogen bins produce significantly different residuals).

EXP-1627: Perfect monotonic trend (Spearman r=1.000) — effective β
  doubles from depleted (0.27) to overflowing (0.72) glycogen.
  10/11 patients agree. Confound warning: proxy correlates with
  insulin delivery state.

EXP-1628: Pre-hypo glycogen predicts recovery — full glycogen →
  43% larger rebound (105 vs 73 mg/dL), r=0.182, p=10⁻¹⁶.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add BiExists, sure/always, bi_equiv, Converges, TOPLAS While-OL rule

Assertion language:
- BiExists: existential quantifier in bi_formula with structural lemmas
- sure(P), always(P): notation matching TOPLAS paper
- bi_equiv: symmetric entailment with refl/sym/trans

Proof rules:
- ol_exists: existential rule + under/pc variants (Rules/Generic.v)

Fix AdLib capture visual tempo — use speed to target ~10 rows/sec

Captured OPL formats (SOP, ROL, etc.) had ticksPerRow=1 at 70Hz refresh,
giving 70 rows/sec visual playback — way too fast. Audio was fine since
it's streamed by the WASM engine independently.

Now computes speed = round(rawRowRate / 10) to target ~10 rows/sec visual
rate, matching typical tracker scrolling speed. BPM stays correct so the
total pattern duration matches the audio duration.

commit.message:
hew-serialize: extract shared SideTableVisitor walker for side-table builders (#889)

Replace three near-identical recursive AST walkers with a single generic
walk_program<V: SideTableVisitor> function. The three build_*_entries
public functions now each supply a small local Visitor impl instead of
carrying a full copy of the traversal skeleton (~350 lines each).

Key design decisions:
- IfStmtOrder enum preserves the existing behavioral difference between
  the assign-kind/method-receiver walkers (ElseBeforeThen) and the
  assign-shape walker (ThenBeforeElse) in Stmt::If traversal.
- ModuleGraphMode enum preserves the two distinct module-graph traversal
  policies: ItemsThenNonRootModules (assign walkers) vs
  ModulesOrProgramItems (method-receiver walker).
- on_assign_stmt hook covers the Stmt::Assign entry-extraction point;
  on_method_call_expr covers the Expr::MethodCall entry-extraction point.
- No schema changes, no public API changes, no behavioral changes.

Net: -512 lines, 130/130 tests pass.

commit.message:
Redesign chat customizations welcome page (#308622)

* Redesign chat customizations welcome page

- Extract welcome page into dedicated AICustomizationWelcomePage class
  and aiCustomizationWelcome.css for better code organization
- Replace old banner + card grid with:
  - 'Analyze Your Project and Configure AI' input box that opens
    /agent-customization in chat with user's description
  - 2x3 category card grid (Agents, Skills, Instructions, Hooks,
    MCP Servers, Plugins) with actionable chip buttons
  - Chips prepopulate chat with specific prompts (e.g. 'Create a
    code review agent', 'Enforce coding style')
- Remove redundant 'Chat Customizations' heading (already in tab)
- Remove redundant 'browse by type' pills (cards serve same purpose)
- Improve chip contrast with visible borders and link-colored text
- Tighten card padding and description sizing for cleaner layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Polish welcome page: bigger input, separator, more copy

- Make input box taller with rounded corners and inner submit button
  styled like a chat input (pill-shaped submit that fills on hover)
- Add section divider line between input area and card grid
- Add 'Or configure individual customizations:' label above grid
- Expand description copy to mention codebase analysis
- Bump card label font-weight to 600 and restore 12px desc size
- Restore card padding to 14px for better breathing room

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rework welcome page: stacked list, better input, centered divider

- Replace 2x3 card grid with full-width stacked category list
  where each row shows icon, label, description, and chip buttons
  inline on a single line
- Input box uses cornerRadius-large, proper padding matching chat
  input style, submit button inside with pill shape
- Separator now shows centered text: 'or configure individually'
  with lines extending on both sides
- Reduce max-width to 680px and remove all excess whitespace
- Remove card/grid CSS entirely

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve welcome page hierarchy and command styling

- Restore strong page intro with 'Chat Customizations' heading and
  descriptive copy from the original welcome page
- Increase overall scale: wider content, larger workflow heading,
  larger description text, and roomier input box
- Break each section into title row, description row, and command row
  instead of compressing everything into a single line
- Restyle example actions as monospace command-like entries such as
  '/create-skill ...' to better communicate they prepopulate chat
- Make category rows roomier with card-like boundaries and better
  visual hierarchy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify welcome page into launcher-style sections

- Replace boxed customization cards with lightweight stacked sections
  separated by subtle rules
- Keep the centered 'or configure individually' separator
- Increase overall scale for the intro, workflow heading, copy, and input
- Make command examples look like real slash commands with plain
  command-link styling instead of pill buttons
- Separate each section into title, description, and command rows for
  clearer hierarchy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert welcome page to original design (keep file separation)

Restore the original welcome page layout from main exactly as it was:
- 'Chat Customizations' heading + subtitle
- 'Configure Your AI' getting-started banner
- Responsive card grid with Browse and Generate with AI buttons

The only structural change kept is the file separation:
welcome page logic lives in aiCustomizationWelcomePage.ts and
welcome CSS lives in media/aiCustomizationWelcome.css.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add centered 'or configure individually' divider between banner and cards

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Configure Your AI banner with chat-style input box

Swap the old getting-started banner for a clean input box styled
like the chat input part: cornerRadius-large, input-background,
submit arrow inside. No highlight borders or extra UI chrome.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix input box focus outlines to match chat input part

Remove the double-outline: the inner monaco-inputbox and the native
input element both had their own focus borders showing through. Force
border/outline to none on both inner elements and let only the outer
container show the single focus border. Use transparent as default
border (matching chat input) and add overflow: hidden to clip inner
outlines at the rounded corners.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix inner input box border by passing transparent inputBoxStyles

The InputBox sets an inline style border via applyStyles() which
cannot be overridden by CSS. Pass inputBorder: transparent and
inputBackground: transparent so the inner box is invisible and
only the outer container provides the border.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor agent customizations welcome page

Split the Agent Customizations welcome page into classic and prompt-launcher variants, add a configuration switch for selecting the implementation, and add component fixtures for the welcome page variants.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Polish customization welcome page variants

Refine the classic and prompt-launcher welcome pages so the workflow entry point is more prominent and the category actions are simpler and better aligned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Swap welcome variants and fix sessions chat prefill

Move the polished workflow-box layout into promptLaunchers (now the default) and restore classic to the origin/main baseline. Add prefillChat callback so the Generate Workflow input works in both the sessions app (via NewChatViewPane.sendQuery) and core VS Code (via workbench.action.chat.open). Enable the getting-started banner in sessions. Fix double-box input styling and card footer alignment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix input focus outline and polish welcome page layout

Replace InputBox widget with plain <input> element to eliminate the focus border that the global .monaco-workbench input[type=text]:focus rule was applying. Move helper text above the input, match input-row rounding to the outer card, and add strong specificity overrides for all focus states.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
